algorithms.title: Monotonic Queue (Deque)
algorithms.description: A monotonic queue (deque) maintains elements in monotonically increasing or decreasing order. Used to find the maximum/minimum in a sliding window in O(N) total time — each element is pushed and popped at most once. The classic application is the Sliding Window Maximum problem.
algorithms.intuition: In a window of size K, if we just added element A and A is larger than some previous elements in the window, those smaller elements can NEVER be the window maximum while A is still in the window (A is better and will stay longer). We can evict them immediately. The deque always holds candidates for the maximum — in decreasing order from front to back. Front = current window maximum.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Use a deque storing indices (not values)."},{"step":2,"description":"For each new element at index i:"},{"step":3,"description":"Remove from BACK: while back element value <= current value (maintain decreasing order)."},{"step":4,"description":"Add current index to BACK."},{"step":5,"description":"Remove from FRONT: if front index is outside window (i - front >= k)."},{"step":6,"description":"Front of deque is the index of the window maximum."}]
algorithms.spaceComplexity: O(K) — deque holds at most K elements

useCases.title: Sliding window maximum/minimum
useCases.desc: Find max/min in each window of size K in O(N)

useCases.title: Jump Game VI
useCases.desc: DP with sliding window max optimization

variants.title: Monotonic Stack
variants.desc: Same idea but elements don't expire — used for next greater element problems

variants.title: Sliding Window Minimum
variants.desc: Same algorithm but pop from back when back value >= current (maintain increasing order)
